name: Vangi Bath
recipeInstructions:
1. Cook the rice using your usual method, ensuring it is dry, fluffy, and light (not sticky).
2. Peel the eggplant and cut into cubes. Add lemon juice and a pinch of salt; let sit for 30 minutes.
3. Heat oil in a saucepan. Fry spices until dark brown. Transfer to a paper napkin-lined plate to drain excess oil.
4. Return oil to the pan, add coconut, and stir until golden/brown. Remove coconut with a slotted spoon.
5. Add more oil to the pan, then eggplant cubes. Stir-fry until about 75% cooked. Remove eggplant and set aside.
6. Blend spices and coconut into a powder using a blender or coffee grinder.
7. In a pot, combine water, lemon juice, turmeric, and salt; bring to a boil.
8. Pour the boiling liquid over the rice. Stir well so the rice adopts the color of the turmeric.
9. Add the spice mixture to the rice. Stir until combined.
10. Squeeze remaining lemon onto the eggplant. Let sit for 1-2 minutes, then add to the rice and stir.
11. Taste and adjust seasoning with salt or additional lemon if needed.
12. Serve Vangi bath with yogurt raita/pachadi.
